subject: Social Science (Political Science)
overall_learning_objectives: Explain the meaning and structure of political parties
Analyse the functions performed by political parties in democracy
Compare different party systems in the world
Differentiate between national and state parties in India
Evaluate the role of political parties in shaping public opinion
Identify major challenges faced by political parties
Propose reforms to strengthen democratic functioning of parties

lesson_plan.title: Meaning and Structure of Political Parties
topics_covered: Components: leaders, active members and followers
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation with examples from Indian politics
student_activities: Students create a concept map of a political party structure

lesson_plan.title: Functions of Political Parties
student_activities: Students match real-life examples with party functions
Write short notes on two important functions

lesson_plan.title: Importance of Political Parties
topics_covered: Role of parties in organizing public opinion
student_activities: Discuss what would happen if there were no political parties

lesson_plan.title: Types of Party Systems
teaching_strategy: Examples from countries like USA, UK and India
student_activities: Prepare a comparison table of party systems

lesson_plan.title: National and State Parties in India
student_activities: Research one regional party and present its role

lesson_plan.title: Challenges to Political Parties
topics_covered: Role of money and muscle power

lesson_plan.title: Reforms and Critical Thinking
student_activities: Suggest reforms to improve political parties

teacher_tip: Encourage students to connect textbook concepts with current political events to better understand the role and functioning of political parties in democracy.
